LAW AND MOTION 302, MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. DEFENDANT STATED THAT THE MATTER WAS RESOLVED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CRITCHFIELD MECHANICAL, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE PRODUCTS LIABILITY AND 402-B FALSE REPRESENTATION CAUSES OF ACTION IS GRANTED. SINCE THE BURDEN OF PROOF WAS SHIFTED, PLAINTIFF WAS REQUIRED TO COME FORWARD WITH EVIDENCE SUPPORTING THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIM AND HAS NOT DONE SO. ACCORDINGLY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THAT CLAIM. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE NEGLIGENCE CLAIM BASED ON TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Off Calendar Oct-02-2008 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF VERNON FARQUHAR'S Motion For Order To Consolidate All Cases In Brayton Group 416 For Trial.
RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - OFF CALENDAR, CASE ADDED TO DEPARTMENT 206'S CALENDAR ON SEPTEMBER 29, 2008. (302/PJM) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Ex Parte Hearing Re: Motion To Consolidate Brayton Group 416
Off Calendar Sep-09-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on for Ex Parte regarding Plaintiffs' Motion to Consolidate Brayton Group 416.
Appearances:
Mr. Fisher, Esq. of Brayton Purcell on behalf of plaintiffs
Various counsel on behalf of Defendants

The Court ordered plaintiff's motion to consolidate in Brayton Group 416 continued from September 18, 2008 to October 2, 2008 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 206. Briefing schedule as follows: Plaintiffs' moving papers and supporting documents to be filed by September 15, 2008, and defendants to file opposition by September 24, 2008. No reply. Plaintiff to put the caption of all cases in Brayton 416 on the notice of motion only. All papers to be filed in the lead case, however parties should identify which case(s) parties are in.
The Court is requiring a chart be accompanied with the papers identifying the following:
Case Name; Trade and years in the trade; Work Location; Illness; Age; Years worked; Smoking History; In Military or not, and any exposure; Other exposure outside main occupation, also para-occupation; List remaining defendants; Work sites, type and extent that there are identical sites.
Plaintiff to prepare order.

Judge Robertson; Clerk: V. Mu; Reporter: Susan Lee (206)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-19-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Adam Koss, Esq. of Buty & Curliano, 510-267-3000 for defendant and moving party Critchfield Mechanical
Laurel Halbany, Esq. of Brayton & Purcell, 415-898-1555 for plaintiff and opposing party Vernon Farquhar

RULING:
MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. DEFENDANT STATED THAT THE MATTER WAS RESOLVED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING.
DEFENDANT CRITCHFIELD MECHANICAL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS AS FOLLOWS:
- When examining Plaintiff's deposition testimony and Plaintiff's answers to the special interrogatories, it is the Court's conclusion that Defendant shifted the burden of proof in that Plaintiff's collective discovery responses do not reflect a basis, beyond Plaintiff's speculation, that the fireproofing material being removed contained asbestos.
- Defendant's objections to the Cantley declaration are overruled and that declaration creates a triable issue of fact along with the Cohen declaration and the motion for summary judgment is denied.
- Plaintiff did not rebut Defendant's evidence that Defendant was only a contractor and the motion for summary adjudication as to the products liability and 402-B false representation causes of action is granted. Since the burden of proof was shifted, Plaintiff was required to come forward with evidence supporting the punitive damages claim and has not done so. Accordingly, summary adjudication is granted as to that claim.
- Summary adjudication is denied as to the negligence claim based on triable issues of fact. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600(302/PJM/PB)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-19-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Alexandra Ozols, Esq. of Lewis, Brisbois, et al, 415-262-8535 for defendant and moving party Sasco Electric
Laurel Halbany, Esq. of Brayton & Purcell, 415-898-1555 for plaintiff and opposing party Vernon Farquhar

RULING:
ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING.
DEFENDANT SASCO ELECTRIC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS AS FOLLOWS:
- The motion is denied. Defendant failed to shift the burden of proof. Plaintiff's special interrogatory responses are not factually devoid and Defendant failed to negate Plaintiff's testimony regarding Defendant's employees disturbing asbestos material at the Fairchild plant in 1982.
- Triable issues of fact also exist based on the Declarations of Plaintiff, Ay and Cohen.
- The objection to the hearsay statement in Plaintiff's declaration is sustained. The objections to the reliance of Cohen and Ay to the presence of "Sasco" at the site is overruled for that is a disputed fact but they are entitled to rely on Plaintiff's version. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600. (302/PJM/PB)

Off Calendar Nov-16-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CHAMPION INDUSTRIAL CONTRACTORS, INC., formerly known as HANSEN'S INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES.
APPEARANCES: Lisa Tandoc, Esq. of Brayton Purcell, LLP 898-1555 for pltf & opposing party; John J. Murray, Esq. of Jackson & Wallace LLP 982-6300 for deft & moving party; (both submitting).
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED BASED UPON THE DECLARATIONS OF ARTS AND FARQUHAR.
THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTIONS FOR STRICT LIABILITY, FALSE REPRESENTATION, AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES.
THE COURT HAS CONSIDERED DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO THE FARQUHAR AND ARTS DECLARATIONS. ALTHOUGH THE ISSUE IS NOT FREE FROM DOUBT, IT IS THE COURT'S CONCLUSION THAT D'AMICO DOES NOT BAR THE IDENTIFICATION OF CHAMPION AS HANSEN. PLAINTIFF'S TESTIMONY REGARDING HANSEN WAS NOT CONCLUSIVE. THE IDENTIFICATION IS FURTHER SUPPORTED BY ARTS. FARQUHAR DOES HAVE A FOUNDATION FOR HIS STATEMENTS REGARDING ASBESTOS INSERTS.
Defendant to submit proposed form of order. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

RULING: Argued and the Court GRANTED the motion. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF. Plaintiff identifies Mr. Zelinsky's painters as using some product to plaster/patch the walls and the Court has determined that there is no evidence that the product presented/utilized and identified by plaintiff had asbestos in it. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-02-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT TODD SHIPYARDS CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: Gilbert L. Purcell, Esq. of Brayton Purcell, LLP 898-1555 for pltf & opposing party; Page Cresswell Perry, Esq. of Yaron & Associates 658-2929 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - denied. Plaintiff's discovery responses are not factually devoid. Defendant may be held liable for the negligent acts of its employees. Here, the record reflects that defendants' employees regularly swept up asbestos materials at the direction of defendant. This conduct exposed plaintiff to asbestos. Plaintiff's declaration also establishes a triable issue of fact and is an excellent illustration of how to provide a foundation for testimony that materials contain asbestos. The objections to the declaration are overruled. Order signed in open court. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
